I EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
I.1 Products covered by this document 
This document covers the Copernicus Marine Service Arctic (ARC) regional observation datasets both 
in near -real time ( ARC NRT) and as a reprocessed time series (ARC MY). Table 1 shows the list of 
products available through the Ocean Colour Thematic Assembly Center (OCTAC) of Copernicus Marine 
Service, the scientific validation of which is provided in this document.  
The ARC chlorophyll -a and the water reflectance datasets from Sentinel -3 OLCI 1 at ~300 m spatial 
resolution are produced in both NRT and MY (2016 -2022), based on the EUMETSAT OLCI V3.0.1 
reprocessing, completed in 2022 as daily Level -3 (L3) and monthly Level -4 (L4) products (see Table 1 
and Table 2). 
The ARC multi-sensor reprocessed time series (MY  years from 1997 to 2022) include surface 
chlorophyll-a concentration (CHL), both as daily L3 and monthly L4, and daily L3 remote -sensing 
reflectances (Rrs) at ~ 4 km spatial resolution as shown in Table 1. This subset of remote sensing 
reflectances (bands 412 -443-490-510-555-670) w as directly extracted from daily Rrs ESA OC -CCI 
(Ocean-Colour Climate Change initiative project ), which uses the OC -CCI processor (version 6.0 2) to 
merge at high resolution MERIS, MODIS-AQUA, SeaWiFS, VIIRS and OLCI data. The ESA OC-CCI Rrs MY 
time series was provided for Copernicus Marine Service by the Plymouth Marine Laboratory (PML) and 
the MY interim production by Brockmann Consult (BC). These data are remapped over the Arctic grid 
and repacked for compliance with the CMEMS format standards. The global quality information of the 
input data is presented in BC's global MY QUID (CMEMS-OC-QUID-009-107to108).

catalog_entry.summary: This document provides a scientific validation of Copernicus Marine Service Arctic regional observation datasets, including near-real time and reprocessed time series products for chlorophyll-a concentration and water reflectance from Sentinel-3 OLCI.

variables.long_name: Remote sensing reflectance in sea water
